content.problemStatement:
A **binary** string SSS of length NNN is said to be almost alternating if it can be converted into an alternating‚Ä†^\dagger‚Ä† string by performing the following operation **at most once:**


Choose two indices LLL and RRR (1‚Č§L‚Č§R‚Č§N1 \leq L \leq R \leq N1‚Č§L‚Č§R‚Č§N).
Then, reverse the substring of SSS from LLL to RRR.

After this operation, SSS will look like S1S2‚Ä¶SL‚ąí1SRSR‚ąí1‚Ä¶SL+1SLSR+1‚Ä¶SNS_1S_2\ldots S_{L-1}S_RS_{R-1}\ldots S_{L+1}S_LS_{R+1}\ldots S_NS1‚ÄčS2‚Äč‚Ä¶SL‚ąí1‚ÄčSR‚ÄčSR‚ąí1‚Äč‚Ä¶SL+1‚ÄčSL‚ÄčSR+1‚Äč‚Ä¶SN‚Äč.
For example, if S=10110110S = \mathtt{10110110}S=10110110, choosing L=3,R=6L = 3, R = 6L=3,R=6 will result in S=101011‚Äĺ10S = \mathtt{10 \underline{1011} 10}S=101011‚Äč10, with the reversed substring underlined.

You are given a binary string SSS. Is it almost alternating?

‚Ä†^\dagger‚Ä† A binary string is said to be alternating if no two adjacent characters are equal. For example, 0,101,\mathtt{0}, \mathtt{101},0,101, and 01010\mathtt{01010}01010 are examples of alternating strings, while 00\mathtt{00}00 and 1010110110\mathtt{1010110110}1010110110 are not.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case will contain a single integer NNN, the length of SSS.
The second line will contain the binary string SSS.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the answer: either `Yes` or `No` depending on whether SSS is almost alternating or not.


Each letter of the output may be printed in either uppercase or lowercase, i.e, the strings `No`, `no`, `NO`, and `nO` will all be treated as equivalent.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚čÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Choosing L=2,R=3L = 2, R = 3L=2,R=3 will result in S=0101S = \mathtt{0101}S=0101, which is alternating.
Test case 222: It can be shown that no matter what, a single reversal cannot make SSS alternating.
Test case 333: SSS is already alternating.
